name: Stock Watchlist Analytics — Collectors Edition
scenario: You're building the analytics backend for a stock watchlist app (Zerodha/Groww style). Traders add stocks and the system must answer: which sector has the most gainers today? What's the average change per sector? Which stocks hit the price target? Which sector has the best overall performance? All solved with Collectors — no manual loops.
description: Analytics system for a stock watchlist using groupingBy, partitioningBy, toMap, summarizingDouble and joining collectors. Replaces all the manual Map-building patterns from Module 6.

core.description: `Stock` class: symbol, sector, price (double), dailyChange (double, % gain/loss), targetPrice (double). Getters + toString.
`avgChangePerSector(List<Stock>)` — returns `Map<String, Double>` of average dailyChange per sector using groupingBy + averagingDouble.
`buildLookup(List<Stock>)` — returns `Map<String, Stock>` of symbol → Stock using toMap.
`sectorStats(List<Stock>)` — returns `Map<String, DoubleSummaryStatistics>` of price stats per sector using groupingBy + summarizingDouble.
`hitTarget(List<Stock>)` — returns `Map<Boolean, List<String>>` (symbol lists) — partitioningBy price >= targetPrice, downstream Collectors.mapping(Stock::getSymbol, toList()).
main() demonstrates all 6 methods. Print formatted output.
Zero manual Map-building loops. All grouping done via Collectors.

bonus.description: Add `topSymbolPerSector(List<Stock>)` using groupingBy + maxBy(dailyChange) — best performer per sector.
Add `sectorReport(List<Stock>)` — returns Map<String, String> where value is a formatted string: 'N stocks, avg change: X%, range: ₹min-₹max'. Uses collectingAndThen or mapping+joining.
Add `joinedSymbolsPerSector(List<Stock>)` using groupingBy + mapping + joining(', ') — produces Map<String, String> of sector → 'SYM1, SYM2, ...'

data_to_use:
Stocks (symbol, sector, price, dailyChange%, targetPrice):
- RELIANCE, Energy, ₹2450, +1.2%, ₹2600
- TCS, IT, ₹3890, +4.1%, ₹4000
- INFY, IT, ₹1560, -3.8%, ₹1700
- WIPRO, IT, ₹412, +5.2%, ₹450
- HDFC, Finance, ₹1678, +0.5%, ₹1800
- SBIN, Finance, ₹620, -1.2%, ₹700
- ZOMATO, Consumer, ₹184, -1.3%, ₹200
- NYKAA, Consumer, ₹156, +3.1%, ₹180
example_output:
=== Sector Groups ===
Consumer: [ZOMATO, NYKAA]
Energy: [RELIANCE]
Finance: [HDFC, SBIN]
IT: [TCS, INFY, WIPRO]

=== Avg Change/Sector ===
Consumer: +0.90%
Energy: +1.20%
Finance: -0.35%
IT: +1.83%

=== Gainers / Losers ===
Gainers (5): [RELIANCE, TCS, WIPRO, HDFC, NYKAA]
Losers (3): [INFY, SBIN, ZOMATO]

=== Hit Price Target ===
Yes: [] | No: [HDFC, INFY, NYKAA, RELIANCE, SBIN, TCS, WIPRO, ZOMATO]

criteria: All 6 methods use correct collectors (30 pts)

ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. The key design: every method body is a single stream.collect() call. hitTarget uses partitioningBy with mapping downstream. sectorStats uses groupingBy + summarizingDouble. No for-loops anywhere.
submission_instructions: Save as WatchlistAnalytics.java. Push to GitHub — this is real financial analytics code using the same patterns used in production trading dashboards.
tips_for_success: hitTarget: partitioningBy(s -> s.getPrice() >= s.getTargetPrice(), Collectors.mapping(Stock::getSymbol, Collectors.toList()))
buildLookup: Collectors.toMap(Stock::getSymbol, s -> s) — no duplicates so no merge function needed
